text.title: Focus Areas Rules and Antipatterns
text.summary: Concrete rules for applying PMBoK 8 focus areas correctly across predictive, agile, and hybrid projects.

rule.statement: Closing is never optional; even purely agile projects require a final retrospective across all sprints and a lessons-learned record.
rule.rationale: Skipping Closing leaves no institutional memory and no formal signal that the project is done.

rule.statement: For agile projects, document the focus-area mapping in the charter (sprint planning = Planning + Executing slice; sprint review = Monitoring and Controlling) so reviewers do not get lost.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit mapping, reviewers apply predictive assumptions and flag missing artefacts that have agile equivalents.

rule.statement: Use "focus area" as the canonical term; never "process group" when working in PMBoK 8 context.
rule.rationale: Mixing PMBoK 6 process group names with PMBoK 8 focus area names causes vocabulary drift and audit confusion.

rule.statement: Abbreviate Monitoring and Controlling consistently as "MnC" within a project; pick one abbreviation and pin it.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent abbreviations (M&C, MnC, MC) create diffs that are hard to read and audit tools that fail silently.

antipattern.description: Listing all 40 non-prescriptive processes in the project plan when only 8-12 apply.
antipattern.reason: Full process lists inflate documentation cost and imply mandatory compliance; cap with explicit tailoring decisions.

antipattern.description: Emitting "Stakeholder phase complete" or "Team phase done" — treating domains as phases.
antipattern.reason: Performance domains are ongoing concerns, not sequential phases; "complete" is never valid for a domain.

antipattern.description: Skipping the Initiating focus area on internal projects ("we already started").
antipattern.reason: No charter or decision record means no traceable rationale for scope and constraints; gap surfaces during audits and change requests.

antipattern.description: Including Procurement under Executing and dropping it when no contractors exist — then adding it back when contractors appear mid-project.
antipattern.reason: Procurement must be evaluated at Initiating/Planning; retroactive addition after kickoff disrupts cost and schedule baselines.
